samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Design an 8-bit unsigned multiplier using pipelining techniques. Implement the design described in the provided Verilog code.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal controlling multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand input signal.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplier input signal.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating if multiplication is enabled.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description></parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_en_out_reg">Register to hold the output enable signal.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplicand.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplier.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[6]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[7]" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary variable for storing partial products.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the first partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the second partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the third partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fourth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fifth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the sixth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_out_reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the final product.</reg>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Resetting the output enable register to 0
                mul_en_out_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                // Assigning the input enable signal to the output enable register
                mul_en_out_reg <= {mul_en_in, mul_en_out_reg[7:1]};
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Resetting the multiplicand register to 0
                mul_a_reg <= 8'd0;
            end else begin
                // Updating the multiplicand register with the input value when the input enable signal is asserted
                if (mul_en_in == 1'b1) begin
                    mul_a_reg <= mul_a;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Resetting the multiplier register to 0
                mul_b_reg <= 8'd0;
            end else begin
                // Updating the multiplier register with the input value when the input enable signal is asserted
                if (mul_en_in == 1'b1) begin
                    mul_b_reg <= mul_b;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Conditional assignment for generating partial products based on the multiplier bits
        assign temp[0] = (mul_b_reg[0]) ? {{8{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg} : 16'd0;
        assign temp[1] = (mul_b_reg[1]) ? {{7{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg, 1'b0} : 16'd0;
        assign temp[2] = (mul_b_reg[2]) ? {{6{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg, 2'b00} : 16'd0;
        assign temp[3] = (mul_b_reg[3]) ? {{5{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg, 3'b000} : 16'd0;
        assign temp[4] = (mul_b_reg[4]) ? {{4{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg, 4'b0000} : 16'd0;
        assign temp[5] = (mul_b_reg[5]) ? {{3{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg, 5'b00000} : 16'd0;
        assign temp[6] = (mul_b_reg[6]) ? {{2{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg, 6'b000000} : 16'd0;
        assign temp[7] = (mul_b_reg[7]) ? {{1{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg, 7'b0000000} : 16'd0;
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Resetting the first partial sum register to 0
                sum[0] <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                // Adding the first two temporary variables to update the first partial sum register
                sum[0] <= temp[0] + temp[1];
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Resetting the second partial sum register to 0
                sum[1] <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                // Adding the next two temporary variables to update the second partial sum register
                sum[1] <= temp[2] + temp[3];
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Resetting the third partial sum register to 0
                sum[2] <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                // Adding the next two temporary variables to update the third partial sum register
                sum[2] <= temp[4] + temp[5];
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Resetting the fourth partial sum register to 0
                sum[3] <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                // Adding the last two temporary variables to update the fourth partial sum register
                sum[3] <= temp[6] + temp[7];
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Resetting the fifth partial sum register to 0
                sum[4] <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                // Adding the first three partial sums to update the fifth partial sum register
                sum[4] <= sum[0] + sum[1] + sum[2];
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Resetting the final product register to 0
                mul_out_reg <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                // Adding the last two partial sums to update the final product register
                mul_out_reg <= sum[4] + sum[3];
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Implements the design described in the provided Verilog code.</implementation>
</module>
samples.tb_log:

%Warning-WIDTH: multi_pipe_8bit.v:21:28: Operator ASSIGNDLY expects 3 bits on the Assign RHS, but Assign RHS's CONST '1'h0' generates 1 bits.
                                       : ... In instance multi_pipe_8bit
   21 |             mul_en_out_reg <= 1'b0;
      |                            ^~
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.

g++  -I.  -MMD -I/usr/share/verilator/include -I/usr/share/verilator/include/vltstd -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TRACE=1 -faligned-new -fcf-protection=none -Wno-bool-operation -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-shadow      -Os -c -o tb.o ../tb.cpp
